functions.description: Current USGS VHP status for all US volcanoes
Latest notice from each USGS observatory
Monitoring instrument stations from all USGS observatories
US volcano catalog from USGS VSC
USGS volcano observatories with boundaries
Recent aviation notices from USGS VSC
Worldwide volcano catalog from USGS VSC
Volcanic SO2 emissions from the Smithsonian GVP
Holocene eruption records from the Smithsonian GVP
Global Pleistocene volcanoes from the Smithsonian GVP
Global Holocene volcanoes from the Smithsonian GVP
Eruptions since 1960 from the Smithsonian GVP
Current daily status summary for elevated volcanoes
Actively monitored volcanoes with current alert levels
Reference: HANS notice type codes and descriptions

Volcanoes currently at elevated alert level
Historical notice archive (VAN, VONA, IS, SR)
Volcanic activity notices from the past month
Volcanoes that have issued HANS notices
US volcanoes with NVEWS threat levels
Volcano Observatory Notices for Aviation (past year)
Historical earthquakes M4.5+ from 2000 onward (cached from FDSN)
Earthquakes from the past 30 days (all magnitudes, live from USGS feed)

ShakeMap ground-motion station observations for recent significant earthquakes
Currently elevated US volcanoes from USGS VSC

functions.macro_definition: CASE  WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 1)) THEN ('Not felt') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 2)) THEN ('Weak') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 3)) THEN ('Weak') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 4)) THEN ('Light') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 5)) THEN ('Moderate') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 6)) THEN ('Strong') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 7)) THEN ('Very strong') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 8)) THEN ('Severe') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 9)) THEN ('Violent') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 10)) THEN ('Extreme') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 11)) THEN ('Extreme') WHEN ((CAST(round(intensity) AS INTEGER) = 12)) THEN ('Extreme') ELSE NULL END

CASE  WHEN ((mag < 2)) THEN ('Micro') WHEN ((mag < 4)) THEN ('Minor') WHEN ((mag < 5)) THEN ('Light') WHEN ((mag < 6)) THEN ('Moderate') WHEN ((mag < 7)) THEN ('Strong') WHEN ((mag < 8)) THEN ('Major') ELSE 'Great' END
CASE  WHEN ((code = 'UNASSIGNED')) THEN (0) WHEN ((code = 'GREEN')) THEN (1) WHEN ((code = 'YELLOW')) THEN (2) WHEN ((code = 'ORANGE')) THEN (3) WHEN ((code = 'RED')) THEN (4) ELSE -1 END

CASE  WHEN (("level" = 'UNASSIGNED')) THEN (0) WHEN (("level" = 'NORMAL')) THEN (1) WHEN (("level" = 'ADVISORY')) THEN (2) WHEN (("level" = 'WATCH')) THEN (3) WHEN (("level" = 'WARNING')) THEN (4) ELSE -1 END

CASE  WHEN ((vei = 0)) THEN ('Non-explosive') WHEN ((vei = 1)) THEN ('Gentle') WHEN ((vei = 2)) THEN ('Explosive') WHEN ((vei = 3)) THEN ('Severe') WHEN ((vei = 4)) THEN ('Cataclysmic') WHEN ((vei = 5)) THEN ('Paroxysmal') WHEN ((vei = 6)) THEN ('Colossal') WHEN ((vei = 7)) THEN ('Super-colossal') WHEN ((vei = 8)) THEN ('Mega-colossal') ELSE NULL END

schemas.comment: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program — real-time and historical seismicity, ShakeMap ground-motion observations (earthquake.usgs.gov)
USGS Volcano Hazard Notification System — US volcano monitoring, alerts, and activity notices (volcanoes.usgs.gov/hans-public/api)
Smithsonian Global Volcanism Program — Holocene/Pleistocene volcano catalog, eruption history, and emissions (volcano.si.edu)
USGS Volcano Science Center — volcano catalogs, observatory status, and monitoring stations (volcanoes.usgs.gov/vsc/api)

tables.comment: ShakeMap ground-motion station observations for recent significant earthquakes
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE earthquakes.shakemap_stations(code VARCHAR NOT NULL, "name" VARCHAR, refid TINYINT, instrumenttype VARCHAR NOT NULL, "source" VARCHAR, network VARCHAR NOT NULL, commtype VARCHAR NOT NULL, "location" VARCHAR NOT NULL, intensity VARCHAR NOT NULL, intensity_flag VARCHAR NOT NULL, intensity_stddev VARCHAR, pga VARCHAR NOT NULL, pgv VARCHAR NOT NULL, distance DOUBLE NOT NULL, elev DOUBLE, vs30 DOUBLE NOT NULL, channels VARCHAR NOT NULL, station_type VARCHAR NOT NULL, nresp VARCHAR, predictions VARCHAR, mmi_from_pgm VARCHAR, distances VARCHAR NOT NULL, event_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, pgm_from_mmi VARCHAR, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(code), CHECK((distance >= 0)), FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES earthquakes.recent(event_id));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Ground-motion observations nearest to epicenter", "sql": "SELECT event_id, name, network, station_type, intensity, distance FROM shakemap_stations ORDER BY event_id, distance"}]

tables.comment: ShakeMap MMI intensity contour lines for recent significant earthquakes
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE earthquakes.shakemap_contours("value" DOUBLE NOT NULL, units VARCHAR NOT NULL, color VARCHAR NOT NULL, weight TINYINT NOT NULL, event_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, CHECK(("value" > 0)), CHECK((weight >= 0)), FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES earthquakes.recent(event_id));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Intensity contour levels by event", "sql": "SELECT event_id, value, color FROM shakemap_contours ORDER BY event_id, value DESC"}]

tables.comment: Earthquakes from the past 30 days (all magnitudes, live from USGS feed)
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE earthquakes.recent(mag DOUBLE, place VARCHAR NOT NULL, "time" TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL, updated TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL, url VARCHAR, detail VARCHAR, felt SMALLINT, cdi DOUBLE, mmi DOUBLE, alert VARCHAR, status VARCHAR NOT NULL, tsunami BOOLEAN NOT NULL, sig SMALLINT NOT NULL, net VARCHAR NOT NULL, code VARCHAR NOT NULL, ids VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, sources VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, "types" VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, nst SMALLINT, dmin DOUBLE, rms DOUBLE, gap DOUBLE, magtype VARCHAR, "type" VARCHAR NOT NULL, title VARCHAR NOT NULL, depth DOUBLE NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, event_id VARCHAR, UNIQUE(event_id), PRIMARY KEY(event_id), CHECK((depth >= -10)), CHECK((sig >= 0)));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Significant earthquakes in the last 30 days", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, depth, time FROM recent WHERE mag >= 5 ORDER BY time DESC"}, {"description": "Earthquake count by magnitude range", "sql": "SELECT floor(mag) AS mag_floor, count(*) AS quake_count FROM recent WHERE mag IS NOT NULL GROUP BY mag_floor ORDER BY mag_floor DESC"}, {"description": "Earthquakes with tsunami flag", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, depth, time FROM recent WHERE tsunami = true ORDER BY time DESC"}, {"description": "Events with a published ShakeMap", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, time FROM recent WHERE list_contains(types, 'shakemap') ORDER BY mag DESC"}]

tables.comment: Historical earthquakes M4.5+ from 2000 onward (cached from FDSN)
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE earthquakes.historical(mag DOUBLE NOT NULL, place VARCHAR NOT NULL, "time" TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL, updated TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL, url VARCHAR, detail VARCHAR, felt INTEGER, cdi DOUBLE, mmi DOUBLE, alert VARCHAR, status VARCHAR NOT NULL, tsunami BOOLEAN NOT NULL, sig SMALLINT NOT NULL, net VARCHAR NOT NULL, code VARCHAR NOT NULL, ids VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, sources VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, "types" VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, nst SMALLINT, dmin DOUBLE, rms DOUBLE, gap DOUBLE, magtype VARCHAR NOT NULL, "type" VARCHAR NOT NULL, title VARCHAR NOT NULL, depth DOUBLE NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, event_id VARCHAR, UNIQUE(event_id), PRIMARY KEY(event_id), CHECK((depth >= -10)), CHECK((sig >= 0)), CHECK((gap BETWEEN 0 AND 360)));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Largest earthquakes since 2000", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, depth, time FROM historical WHERE mag >= 8 ORDER BY mag DESC"}, {"description": "Annual earthquake count and average magnitude", "sql": "SELECT extract(year FROM time) AS year, count(*) AS quake_count, round(avg(mag), 1) AS avg_mag FROM historical GROUP BY year ORDER BY year"}]

tables.comment: Volcano Observatory Notices for Aviation (past year)
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.vonas(sent_utc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_identifier VARCHAR, notice_type_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_fullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, volcano_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, region VARCHAR NOT NULL, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, volcano_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_image_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, nvews_threat VARCHAR NOT NULL, synopsis_complete VARCHAR NOT NULL, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, vona_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, vona_data VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(notice_identifier), PRIMARY KEY(notice_identifier), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr), FOREIGN KEY (notice_type_cd) REFERENCES hans.notice_types(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Recent aviation color code changes", "sql": "SELECT volcano_name, alert_level, color_code, sent_utc FROM vonas ORDER BY sent_utc DESC"}]

tables.comment: Reference: HANS notice type codes and descriptions
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.notice_types(noticecategory VARCHAR NOT NULL, noticetype VARCHAR NOT NULL, description VARCHAR NOT NULL, noticetypecd VARCHAR, UNIQUE(noticetypecd), UNIQUE(noticetype), PRIMARY KEY(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Notice type codes and descriptions", "sql": "SELECT noticetypecd, noticetype, description, noticecategory FROM notice_types"}]

tables.comment: Historical notice archive (VAN, VONA, IS, SR)
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.research_notices(apiindex SMALLINT, sent_utc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, volcano_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_identifier VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_type_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, synopsis VARCHAR, UNIQUE(apiindex), PRIMARY KEY(apiindex), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr), FOREIGN KEY (notice_type_cd) REFERENCES hans.notice_types(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Alert history for Kilauea", "sql": "SELECT sent_utc, alert_level, color_code, notice_type_cd FROM research_notices WHERE volcano_name = 'Kilauea' ORDER BY sent_utc DESC"}, {"description": "Most frequently notified volcanoes", "sql": "SELECT volcano_name, count(*) AS notice_count FROM research_notices GROUP BY volcano_name ORDER BY notice_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: USGS volcano observatories
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.observatories(obsabbr VARCHAR, obsname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obsfullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obsemail VARCHAR NOT NULL, UNIQUE(obsabbr), UNIQUE(obsname), UNIQUE(obsfullname), PRIMARY KEY(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "All USGS volcano observatories", "sql": "SELECT obsabbr, obsfullname, obsemail FROM observatories"}]

tables.comment: Actively monitored volcanoes with current alert levels
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.monitored_volcanoes(volcano_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER, sent_utc TIMESTAMP, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_fullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_type_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_identifier VARCHAR, notice_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_data VARCHAR NOT NULL, UNIQUE(volcano_cd), UNIQUE(volcano_name), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr), FOREIGN KEY (notice_type_cd) REFERENCES hans.notice_types(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Volcanoes above normal alert level", "sql": "SELECT volcano_name, alert_level, color_code, obs_fullname FROM monitored_volcanoes WHERE alert_level != 'NORMAL' ORDER BY sent_utc DESC"}]

tables.comment: Volcanoes currently at elevated alert level
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.elevated_volcanoes(obs_fullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, notice_type_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_identifier VARCHAR NOT NULL, sent_utc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_data VARCHAR NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr), FOREIGN KEY (notice_type_cd) REFERENCES hans.notice_types(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Currently elevated volcanoes with alert details", "sql": "SELECT volcano_name, alert_level, color_code, sent_utc FROM elevated_volcanoes ORDER BY sent_utc DESC"}]

tables.comment: Volcanoes that have issued HANS notices
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.volcanoes_with_notices(obsabbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, volccd VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcname VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcurl VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER, imgurl VARCHAR, threat VARCHAR, UNIQUE(volccd), UNIQUE(volcname), FOREIGN KEY (obsabbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Volcanoes with notices by observatory", "sql": "SELECT obsabbr, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM volcanoes_with_notices GROUP BY obsabbr ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: US volcanoes with NVEWS threat levels
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.us_volcanoes(obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_fullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_email VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER, volcano_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, region VARCHAR NOT NULL, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, elevation_meters SMALLINT NOT NULL, boilerplate VARCHAR, volcano_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano_image_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, nvews_threat VARCHAR NOT NULL, icao_coordinates VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vnum), UNIQUE(volcano_cd), UNIQUE(volcano_name), PRIMARY KEY(vnum), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Very high threat US volcanoes", "sql": "SELECT volcano_name, region, elevation_meters FROM us_volcanoes WHERE nvews_threat = 'Very High Threat' ORDER BY elevation_meters DESC"}, {"description": "US volcanoes by observatory", "sql": "SELECT obs_fullname, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM us_volcanoes GROUP BY obs_fullname ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Volcanic activity notices from the past month
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.recent_notices(sent_utc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, notice_identifier VARCHAR, volcanoes VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_type_title VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_type_cd VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_category VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_fullname VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_data VARCHAR NOT NULL, UNIQUE(notice_identifier), PRIMARY KEY(notice_identifier), FOREIGN KEY (obs_abbr) REFERENCES hans.observatories(obsabbr), FOREIGN KEY (notice_type_cd) REFERENCES hans.notice_types(noticetypecd));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Recent notices by type", "sql": "SELECT notice_type_title, count(*) AS notice_count FROM recent_notices GROUP BY notice_type_title ORDER BY notice_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Current daily status summary for elevated volcanoes
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE hans.daily_summary(noticeid VARCHAR NOT NULL, vname VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, synopsis VARCHAR NOT NULL, alertlevel VARCHAR NOT NULL, colorcode VARCHAR NOT NULL, sentrfc VARCHAR NOT NULL, statusrfc VARCHAR NOT NULL, prevalertlevel VARCHAR, prevcolorcode VARCHAR, FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Current elevated volcano status summary", "sql": "SELECT vname, alertlevel, colorcode, synopsis FROM daily_summary"}]

tables.comment: Eruptions since 1960 from the Smithsonian GVP
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE smithsonian.recent_eruptions(vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, vei TINYINT NOT NULL, start_date DATE, start_year SMALLINT NOT NULL, start_month TINYINT, start_day TINYINT, end_date DATE, end_year SMALLINT, end_month TINYINT, end_day TINYINT, continuing BOOLEAN NOT NULL, latitude_decimal DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude_decimal DOUBLE NOT NULL, activity_id SMALLINT, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(activity_id), PRIMARY KEY(activity_id), CHECK((vei BETWEEN 0 AND 8)), CHECK((latitude_decimal BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude_decimal BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Currently continuing eruptions", "sql": "SELECT name, start_year, vei FROM recent_eruptions WHERE continuing = true ORDER BY start_year DESC"}, {"description": "Eruption count by VEI since 1960", "sql": "SELECT vei, count(*) AS eruption_count FROM recent_eruptions GROUP BY vei ORDER BY vei DESC"}]

tables.comment: Global Holocene volcanoes from the Smithsonian GVP
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, landform VARCHAR NOT NULL, primary_type VARCHAR, last_eruption_year SMALLINT, country VARCHAR NOT NULL, region VARCHAR NOT NULL, subregion VARCHAR NOT NULL, geological_summary VARCHAR, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, elevation SMALLINT NOT NULL, tectonic_setting VARCHAR, geologic_epoch VARCHAR NOT NULL, evidence_category VARCHAR, photo_link VARCHAR, photo_caption VARCHAR, photo_credit VARCHAR, rock_type VARCHAR, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vnum), PRIMARY KEY(vnum), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Stratovolcano count by country", "sql": "SELECT country, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM holocene_volcanoes WHERE primary_type = 'Stratovolcano' GROUP BY country ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}, {"description": "US volcanoes with most recent eruptions", "sql": "SELECT name, elevation, last_eruption_year FROM holocene_volcanoes WHERE country = 'United States' AND last_eruption_year IS NOT NULL ORDER BY last_eruption_year DESC"}]

tables.comment: Global Pleistocene volcanoes from the Smithsonian GVP
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE smithsonian.pleistocene_volcanoes(vnum INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, landform VARCHAR NOT NULL, primary_type VARCHAR, country VARCHAR NOT NULL, region VARCHAR NOT NULL, subregion VARCHAR NOT NULL, geological_summary VARCHAR, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, elevation SMALLINT, geologic_epoch VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vnum), PRIMARY KEY(vnum), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Pleistocene volcanoes by region", "sql": "SELECT region, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM pleistocene_volcanoes GROUP BY region ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Holocene eruption records from the Smithsonian GVP
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE smithsonian.eruptions(vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, eruption_number SMALLINT NOT NULL, activity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL, vei TINYINT, vei_modifier VARCHAR, activity_area VARCHAR, activity_unit VARCHAR, evidence_method VARCHAR, start_year_modifier VARCHAR, start_year INTEGER NOT NULL, start_year_uncertainty SMALLINT, start_day_modifier VARCHAR, start_month TINYINT, start_day TINYINT, start_day_uncertainty SMALLINT, end_year_modifier VARCHAR, end_year SMALLINT, end_year_uncertainty TINYINT, end_day_modifier VARCHAR, end_month TINYINT, end_day TINYINT, end_day_uncertainty SMALLINT, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, start_date_tz TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, duration INTERVAL, CHECK((vei BETWEEN 0 AND 8)), CHECK((start_month BETWEEN 0 AND 12)), CHECK((start_day BETWEEN 0 AND 31)), CHECK((end_month BETWEEN 0 AND 12)), CHECK((end_day BETWEEN 0 AND 31)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Largest eruptions in recorded history (VEI 6+)", "sql": "SELECT name, vei, start_year FROM eruptions WHERE vei >= 6 ORDER BY vei DESC, start_year"}, {"description": "Longest recorded eruptions", "sql": "SELECT name, start_year, end_year, duration FROM eruptions WHERE duration IS NOT NULL ORDER BY duration DESC"}, {"description": "Eruption frequency by millennium", "sql": "SELECT (start_year / 1000) * 1000 AS millennium, count(*) AS eruption_count FROM eruptions WHERE start_year >= -10000 GROUP BY millennium ORDER BY millennium"}]

tables.comment: Volcanic SO2 emissions from the Smithsonian GVP
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE smithsonian.emissions(vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, so2_kilotons DOUBLE NOT NULL, start_date DATE NOT NULL, end_date DATE NOT NULL, emission_id SMALLINT, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(emission_id), PRIMARY KEY(emission_id), CHECK((so2_kilotons > 0)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Largest SO2 emitters", "sql": "SELECT name, sum(so2_kilotons) AS total_so2_kt FROM emissions GROUP BY name ORDER BY total_so2_kt DESC"}, {"description": "Annual SO2 emissions trend", "sql": "SELECT extract(year FROM start_date) AS year, sum(so2_kilotons) AS total_so2_kt FROM emissions GROUP BY year ORDER BY year"}]

tables.comment: Recent aviation notices from USGS VSC
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.vonas(vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, "name" VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs VARCHAR, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, nvews_threat VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_synopsis VARCHAR NOT NULL, sent_utc DATE NOT NULL, notice_id VARCHAR, notice_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_data VARCHAR NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Aviation notices by alert level", "sql": "SELECT alert_level, count(*) AS notice_count FROM vonas GROUP BY alert_level ORDER BY notice_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Current USGS VHP status for all US volcanoes
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.vhp_status(vname VARCHAR NOT NULL, lat DOUBLE NOT NULL, long DOUBLE NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER, volcanocd VARCHAR NOT NULL, vurl VARCHAR NOT NULL, vimage VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs VARCHAR NOT NULL, region VARCHAR NOT NULL, noticeid VARCHAR, noticesynopsis VARCHAR, alertlevel VARCHAR NOT NULL, colorcode VARCHAR NOT NULL, statusiconurl VARCHAR NOT NULL, alertdate TIMESTAMP, colordate TIMESTAMP, nvewsthreat VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vname), UNIQUE(volcanocd), UNIQUE(vurl), CHECK((lat BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((long BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Volcanoes at elevated alert", "sql": "SELECT vname, alertlevel, colorcode, nvewsthreat FROM vhp_status WHERE alertlevel NOT IN ('NORMAL', 'UNASSIGNED') ORDER BY alertdate DESC"}]

tables.comment: USGS volcano observatories with boundaries
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.observatories(obsabbr VARCHAR, observatory VARCHAR NOT NULL, lat DOUBLE NOT NULL, long DOUBLE NOT NULL, boundary VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(obsabbr), UNIQUE(observatory), PRIMARY KEY(obsabbr), CHECK((lat BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((long BETWEEN -180 AND 180)));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Observatory locations", "sql": "SELECT obsabbr, observatory, lat, long FROM observatories"}]

tables.comment: Worldwide volcano catalog from USGS VSC
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.worldwide_volcanoes(vnum INTEGER, vname VARCHAR NOT NULL, country VARCHAR NOT NULL, subregion VARCHAR, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, elevation_m DOUBLE NOT NULL, obsabbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, webpage VARCHAR NOT NULL, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(webpage), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obsabbr) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Volcanoes by country", "sql": "SELECT country, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM worldwide_volcanoes GROUP BY country ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Monitoring instrument stations from all USGS observatories
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.monitoring_stations(station VARCHAR NOT NULL, stationid INTEGER, active VARCHAR NOT NULL, site_name VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, permit VARCHAR NOT NULL, obs VARCHAR NOT NULL, volcano VARCHAR NOT NULL, distancekm DOUBLE NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, nvews TINYINT NOT NULL, nvews_threat_rank TINYINT, nvews_score SMALLINT NOT NULL, nvews_radius_km TINYINT NOT NULL, nvews_polygon VARCHAR, nvews_34_ind VARCHAR NOT NULL, archives VARCHAR, landmanagers VARCHAR, landowners VARCHAR, landunits VARCHAR, wilderness VARCHAR, networks VARCHAR, operators VARCHAR, processors VARCHAR, sponsors VARCHAR, sensors VARCHAR, hasdigitaltelemetry VARCHAR, hasanalogtelemetry VARCHAR, output VARCHAR, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(station), UNIQUE(stationid), PRIMARY KEY(stationid), CHECK((distancekm >= 0)), CHECK((nvews_score >= 0)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Station count per volcano", "sql": "SELECT volcano, count(*) AS station_count FROM monitoring_stations GROUP BY volcano ORDER BY station_count DESC"}, {"description": "Stations closest to their monitored volcano", "sql": "SELECT station, volcano, distancekm, obs FROM monitoring_stations ORDER BY distancekm"}]

tables.comment: Latest notice from each USGS observatory
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.latest_notices(obs VARCHAR, notice_title VARCHAR NOT NULL, sent_utc DATE NOT NULL, notice_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, notice_url VARCHAR NOT NULL, alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, highest_alert_level VARCHAR NOT NULL, highest_color_code VARCHAR NOT NULL, UNIQUE(obs), UNIQUE(notice_id), PRIMARY KEY(obs), FOREIGN KEY (obs) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Latest notice from each observatory", "sql": "SELECT obs, notice_title, alert_level, color_code, sent_utc FROM latest_notices ORDER BY sent_utc DESC"}]

tables.comment: US volcano catalog from USGS VSC
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.us_volcanoes(vnum INTEGER, volcanocd VARCHAR, vname VARCHAR NOT NULL, country VARCHAR, subregion VARCHAR NOT NULL, latitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, longitude DOUBLE NOT NULL, elevation_m DOUBLE NOT NULL, obsabbr VARCHAR NOT NULL, webpage VARCHAR NOT NULL, nvews TINYINT, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vname), UNIQUE(webpage), CHECK((latitude BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((longitude BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obsabbr) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "US volcanoes by observatory", "sql": "SELECT obsabbr, count(*) AS volcano_count FROM us_volcanoes GROUP BY obsabbr ORDER BY volcano_count DESC"}]

tables.comment: Currently elevated US volcanoes from USGS VSC
tables.sql: CREATE TABLE vsc.elevated_volcanoes(noticeid VARCHAR NOT NULL, vname VARCHAR NOT NULL, vnum INTEGER NOT NULL, volcanocd VARCHAR NOT NULL, nvewsthreat VARCHAR NOT NULL, lat DOUBLE NOT NULL, long DOUBLE NOT NULL, noticesynopsis VARCHAR, obs VARCHAR NOT NULL, alertlevel VARCHAR NOT NULL, colorcode VARCHAR NOT NULL, sentutc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, alertdate TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, newdate VARCHAR NOT NULL, newtime TIME NOT NULL, alertlevelprev VARCHAR, colorcodeprev VARCHAR, codechangedate TIMESTAMP, noticeurl VARCHAR NOT NULL, noticedata VARCHAR NOT NULL, noticesectiondata VARCHAR, geom GEOMETRY NOT NULL, UNIQUE(vnum), UNIQUE(vname), CHECK((lat BETWEEN -90 AND 90)), CHECK((long BETWEEN -180 AND 180)), FOREIGN KEY (vnum) REFERENCES smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes(vnum), FOREIGN KEY (obs) REFERENCES vsc.observatories(obsabbr));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Currently elevated US volcanoes", "sql": "SELECT vname, alertlevel, colorcode, sentutc FROM elevated_volcanoes ORDER BY sentutc DESC"}]

views.comment: Earthquakes from the last 30 days that received Did You Feel It reports
views.sql: CREATE VIEW earthquakes.felt AS SELECT event_id, place, mag, depth, "time", felt, cdi, mmi, alert, sig, url, geom FROM earthquakes.recent WHERE ((felt IS NOT NULL) AND (felt > 0));

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Most widely felt earthquakes", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, felt, cdi, time FROM felt ORDER BY felt DESC"}]

views.comment: Earthquakes magnitude 5+ from the last 30 days
views.sql: CREATE VIEW earthquakes.significant AS SELECT event_id, place, mag, magtype, depth, "time", updated, felt, cdi, mmi, alert, tsunami, sig, url, geom FROM earthquakes.recent WHERE (mag >= 5);

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Significant earthquakes ordered by magnitude", "sql": "SELECT place, mag, depth, time FROM significant ORDER BY mag DESC"}]

views.comment: Per-volcano eruption summary: count, max VEI, date range, and number of large (VEI 4+) eruptions
views.sql: CREATE VIEW smithsonian.eruption_statistics AS SELECT vnum, "name", count_star() AS eruption_count, max(vei) AS max_vei, min(start_year) AS earliest_year, max(start_year) AS latest_year, count_star() FILTER (WHERE (vei >= 4)) AS large_eruptions FROM smithsonian.eruptions GROUP BY vnum, "name";

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "Volcanoes with the most VEI 4+ eruptions", "sql": "SELECT name, eruption_count, max_vei, large_eruptions FROM eruption_statistics WHERE large_eruptions > 0 ORDER BY large_eruptions DESC"}, {"description": "Most frequently erupting volcanoes", "sql": "SELECT name, eruption_count, earliest_year, latest_year FROM eruption_statistics ORDER BY eruption_count DESC"}]

views.comment: Eruptions enriched with volcano country, region, and type from the Holocene catalog
views.sql: CREATE VIEW smithsonian.eruption_history AS SELECT e.vnum, e."name", v.country, v.region, v.primary_type, e.eruption_number, e.activity_type, e.vei, e.start_year, e.start_month, e.start_day, e.end_year, e.end_month, e.end_day, e.start_date, e.end_date, e.start_date_tz, e.duration, e.evidence_method, e.geom FROM smithsonian.eruptions AS e INNER JOIN smithsonian.holocene_volcanoes AS v USING (vnum);

tags.vgi.example_queries: [{"description": "VEI 6+ eruptions with country and volcano type", "sql": "SELECT name, country, primary_type, vei, start_year FROM eruption_history WHERE vei >= 6 ORDER BY start_year"}, {"description": "Eruption count by country", "sql": "SELECT country, count(*) AS eruption_count FROM eruption_history GROUP BY country ORDER BY eruption_count DESC"}]
